response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong polyglot background (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, Java) and genuine geospatial experience through GISCollective and Green Map work. However, the role demands modern C++ or Rust as the primary skill, and the resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments over a decade ago (2014-2015). No Rust experience listed, no recent systems-level work on low-latency pipelines in C++, and no navigation/routing tile experience. The geospatial background and low-latency DLang work at Sociomantic are tangentially relevant but unlikely to substitute for current C++ proficiency in a routing tiling pipeline.
strengths: Genuine geospatial domain experience via GISCollective and Open Green Map platforms
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Sociomantic (millions of req/sec, async I/O, fibers)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing advocacy
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over 10 years old; no Rust experience listed
No navigation/routing pipeline or map tiling background
Recent work is primarily TypeScript/Node.js full-stack, not systems-level C++/Rust
